implement.note: three client.ts clobber sites fixed (streamAgent/oneShot/runAgent prefer-accumulation, final-frame fallback only when nothing streamed) + the chat.ts final marker pass DEVIATION (push() return was discarded, keeping only the 25-char hold-back tail - the observed truncation length; acceptance unreachable without it) + the review finding fix (provider-error scan gated on the nothing-streamed shape, chat.ts + build.ts)

test.detail: full workspace suite green post-fix: shared 32, api 962/1-skip, web 113; after the finding fix: agents+llm+streaming 181/181 + typecheck 0. Rewritten chat-lifecycle test asserts complete.result === join(text_chunks) === persisted message; nothing-streamed fallback covered; streamed-answer-mentioning-429 regression covered

findings.summary: F20 widened the provider-error-as-result scan from the 25-char tail to the whole answer - legitimate prose mentioning '429'/'401' would be discarded as a provider outage
findings.resolution: fixed in-slice regression-test-first: scan gated on the nothing-streamed fallback shape in chat.ts + build.ts

adversarialReview.evidence: reviewer independently ran agents/llm/streaming (180 green pre-finding-fix), gate:chokepoint exit 0, typecheck exit 0; verified the chat.ts deviation is sound AND necessary (single-push marker semantics: disjoint slices, no duplication, no marker leak; also fixes findings extraction which pre-F20 only saw the tail); verified anonymise ordering, abort semantics, and all downstream result.text consumers improve

codexSliceReview.detail: feature profile: codex-slice runs on security-boundary slices only; S3 is llm/-area (significance-labelled) but not an auth/egress boundary change - the fresh-context review covered it; codex also unavailable on this host (not logged in)
